pre_game.message: Hey there, I'm I. It’s a bit surreal being in here waiting for the game to start, isn't it? What's your name, and how are you feeling about all of this?
Hey I, I'm A. It definitely feels a bit strange, but I'm mostly just focused and ready to get going—I've got people at home counting on me. How are you holding up?
I’m doing alright, just trying to keep my head in the game. I’m in the same boat with people back home counting on me, so I’m pretty determined to walk away with something.
I hear that. It definitely adds some pressure when it's not just about yourself. Have you thought much about how you're going to approach the game?
I’m still figuring it out, but I think being observant and trying to work with people is the way to go—at least at first. What about you?
I’m on the same page. I’d rather find some common ground and work together than have everyone just look out for themselves from the start.
Glad to hear it. It's definitely better to have an ally in here than to try and do it all alone.
For sure. It’s a lot easier to navigate this if we’ve got each other’s backs.
Exactly. Let's keep that in mind when the game starts; we'll be much stronger if we stick together.
Sounds like a plan. I'm going to see who else is here before things get started, but let's definitely stay in touch. Good luck.
Sounds good, A. Good luck talking to the others, and let's definitely catch up once things get rolling.

post_game.message: I chose to take $10,000 because it was the fair share, and I'm glad we all did the same for a perfect outcome.
I agree that it was the best outcome for everyone. By choosing the fair share, we ensured that we both walked away with the maximum possible benefit through cooperation.
